name: gpu_diffusion
title: Image generation
short_description: Diffusion image generation on the graphics processor.

structured_text.description: Generates images with a diffusion model on the graphics processor, running a fixed number of denoising steps per image at a set resolution. The model and settings are identical on every machine, so the result reflects sustained accelerated compute and memory throughput.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is the number of images produced per minute, reported as items per minute.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. More images per minute means less waiting when generating or iterating on visual ideas.
structured_text.reasoning: Diffusion image generation is one of the most common local machine learning workloads, and its denoising loop is a heavy, repeatable test of accelerated inference.

domain_gaming.explanation: Generated textures and assets are used in some game pipelines.

domain_office.explanation: Generated imagery appears in presentations and documents.

domain_software_development.explanation: Developers integrate image generation into applications they build.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Image generation is now a regular part of the design workflow.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Generated frames and elements are pulled into edited video.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Generated textures and concepts feed 3D production.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work makes little use of image generation.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Engineering work rarely needs generated imagery.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Generative models support some scientific imaging tasks.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Generated images are used to augment training data sets.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Diffusion generation is a core local inference workload.

domain_data_science.explanation: Synthetic image data supports some analytics and modelling work.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Hosted generation services run this workload continuously for many users.
